artifacts.title: Guidelines for management of iron deficiency anaemia in adults
assertions: History should seek overt blood loss including menstruation and epistaxis, blood donation, inadequate diet, NSAID use and previous gastrointestinal resection or bypass.
Evaluation and treatment response require iron studies, coeliac and gastrointestinal context, medication and follow-up information; this package does not diagnose the cause.

artifacts.title: Suspected cancer: anaemia-related recommendations
assertions: Iron-deficiency anaemia and selected age or symptom combinations require FIT or referral consideration; pallor, persistent fatigue, fever, infection, bruising, bleeding and petechiae can warrant urgent blood count assessment.

artifacts.title: Guideline on ferritin concentrations to assess iron status
assertions: Ferritin is an iron-store marker whose interpretation must consider infection and inflammation; raw result, reference context and inflammatory markers should be preserved.

artifacts.title: STOM anaemia terminology and MRCM summary
assertions: FHIR lookup confirmed active concepts for anaemia, iron-deficiency anaemia, macrocytic anaemia, B12- and folate-deficiency megaloblastic anaemia, blood-loss anaemia, chronic-disease anaemia and low haemoglobin.
MRCM supports provisional semantic binding only and does not establish cause or urgency.
